403.description: Not allowed to edit this contact
Not allowed to edit this contact

200.description: Family created and caller auto-joined as Owner

parameters.name: family_id
parameters.description: Family id (must match the active X-Family-Id)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: 1-based page index. Defaults to 1; values below 1 are clamped.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: One of 50 / 100 / 200 / 500. Anything else falls back to 50.

parameters.name: actor_user_id
parameters.description: Restrict to rows written by this user.

401.description: Path family_id does not match active family

200.description: Invite accepted, membership inserted, session refreshed

401.description: No session or invite token invalid

422.description: Invite belongs to a different email

403.description: Not allowed to edit this person

404.description: Person not found in this family

403.description: Not allowed to edit contacts on this person

404.description: Person not found in this family

200.description: Confirmation link sent to current email

409.description: New email already in use by another account

EdgePair.description:
A parent → child edge with kind metadata.

`a` is the child, `b` is the parent — matches the historical
`EdgePair` orientation the FE layout code expects. `kind` powers
the drawer's inline "change parent-link kind" affordance.

FieldViolation.description:
A single field validation failure on the wire.

- `path`: JSON Pointer or query/header path to the offending field (e.g.
  `/email`, `/body/family_name`, `/headers/x-family-id`).
- `code`: stable i18n key (e.g. `validation.email_invalid`,
  `validation.string_too_long`). FE looks this up in en/de catalogs.
- `message`: English fallback so non-FE clients still get a readable
  error.
- `params`: named placeholders FE substitutes into the localized
  message. Always serialized (even when empty) so FE clients don't have
  to guard `params?.max`. Use e.g. `{"max": 100, "actual": 105}`.

Pagination.description: Cursor-based pagination block. `next_cursor` is `None` on the last page.

PartnerEdge.description:
A partnership edge with id, kind, and lifecycle dates.

The id is what `PATCH /partnerships/{id}` keys on — without it the
FE has no way to edit or end an existing partnership inline.

PersonUpdateReq.description:
Partial update. Every field is optional; only `Some(_)` fields overwrite
the corresponding column. An empty body is rejected as `validation.value_required`.

ResponseMeta.description:
Per-response metadata. Currently carries pagination cursors, the
`request_id` for tracing-friendly client diagnostics, and any soft
validation `warnings` attached by the handler.

warnings.description:
Soft warnings. Serialized only when non-empty so the wire shape
stays unchanged for handlers that don't produce any.

TreeNode.description:
One person plus their relationship adjacency lists.

`parent_ids` and `partner_ids` are denormalized so the FE's SVG layout
doesn't have to join `parent_edges` / `partner_edges` itself.

linked_user_id.description:
Set when this person row maps to a `users.id`. Used by the FE to
resolve "the signed-in user's own node" so the tree can auto-center
on them on first load.

UpcomingEvent.description:
One enumerated future date.

`kind` is one of `birthday`, `wedding_anniversary`, `death_anniversary`.
`person_id` is set for `birthday` and `death_anniversary`;
`partnership_id` is set for `wedding_anniversary` with both partner
ids surfaced so the FE can deep-link the row's click to one of them
(it centers on `partner_a_id` and opens that person's drawer; the
tree-edge between the two partners conveys the relationship).
`label` is pre-rendered server-side so the FE doesn't need to
re-translate the "Nth birthday" phrasing per locale (i18n stays
on `upcoming.kinds.*`; the name + N come from the API).

filter.description:
One of `all` (default), `birthday`, `anniversary`. Unknown
values fall through to `all` — the FE never sends arbitrary
strings, but a hand-crafted URL stays gracefully degraded.

limit.description:
1..=200, default 20. Clamped to the cap so callers can't
exhaust memory by passing `limit=999999`.

Warning.description:
Soft-validation warning.

Surfaced under `meta.warnings` on the success envelope when the
caller's input violates a heuristic rule that we don't want to reject
outright (e.g. parent-child age gap < 14 years). The `code` is a
stable i18n key (`warning.<snake_case>`); `path` is an optional JSON
Pointer to the relevant field.
